codeReview.summary: Comprehensive integration tests for DockerSandbox that thoroughly cover all 4 validation assertions (VAL-DOCKER-001 through VAL-DOCKER-004). The worker added 12 new integration tests to the existing 9 unit tests in src/swe/docker_sandbox.rs. Tests cover container lifecycle, command execution (success/failure/timeout), file operations (write/read/nested paths), cleanup verification, repository cloning, and resource limits. All 21 tests pass and properly clean up Docker containers.

tests: test_container_creation_and_destruction - Verifies container is created and exists
test_repo_cloning_and_checkout - Tests container creation with git operations
test_container_resource_limits - Verifies memory limits are configured
VAL-DOCKER-001: Container Creation.notes: Tests verify container starts with correct image, network=host, memory=32g limits. Container existence is verified via docker ps filtering.

tests: test_cleanup_on_drop - Tests Drop trait cleanup synchronization
VAL-DOCKER-002: Container Destruction.notes: Tests verify containers are removed via both explicit destroy() call and automatic Drop cleanup. Worker improved Drop implementation to use synchronous Command::new for reliability. Container existence is verified before and after destruction.

tests: test_command_execution_failure - Non-zero exit codes, error output
VAL-DOCKER-003: Command Execution.notes: Comprehensive coverage of command execution including success paths, failure paths, timeout handling (1s timeout on 5s sleep), and special character escaping. All aspects of SandboxOutput are verified.

tests: test_file_write_and_read - Basic file write and content verification
test_read_nonexistent_file - Error handling for missing files
test_multiple_file_operations - Multiple files in same container
VAL-DOCKER-004: File Operations.notes: Tests cover write_file() and read_file() methods with various scenarios including nested paths that require automatic directory creation. Content integrity is verified with exact string matching.

strengths: All tests use proper async/await patterns with tokio
Docker availability checks skip tests gracefully when Docker is unavailable
Unique container names using UUID suffix prevent parallel test collisions
Proper helper functions for container existence checking
Tests are independent and can run in parallel
Comprehensive cleanup verification using docker ps -a filtering
improvements: The worker also improved the production code by changing container name generation from millisecond timestamps (collision-prone) to UUID suffixes
Drop implementation was improved to use synchronous Command instead of async for reliable cleanup

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the rust-test-worker skill procedure precisely. All steps were followed: understanding requirements, planning test coverage, writing tests in TDD pattern, ensuring Docker test safety, running tests incrementally, and verifying no container leaks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true with no deviations. All verification commands were run including docker ps -a check for lingering containers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Tests use the project's established patterns: #[tokio::test] for async tests, anyhow::Result for error handling, tracing for logging, and proper path validation using validate_file_path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/swe/docker_sandbox.rs tests module follows same patterns as existing unit tests (test_image_for_language, test_truncate_*, etc.).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered and fixed a production bug: millisecond timestamps caused container name collisions in parallel tests. Changed to UUID suffix for uniqueness.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Container name generation changed from timestamps to UUID in src/swe/docker_sandbox.rs:104-105. This improvement benefits both tests and production usage.

summary: The docker-sandbox-tests feature is fully implemented and passes review. All 4 validation assertions (VAL-DOCKER-001 through VAL-DOCKER-004) are comprehensively covered by 12 new integration tests. The worker also made valuable production code improvements: 1) Changed container name generation to use UUID suffixes preventing parallel test collisions, and 2) Improved Drop implementation for more reliable cleanup. All 21 tests pass, no containers leak, clippy passes with no warnings. The tests properly use Docker availability guards to skip gracefully when Docker is unavailable. This is a high-quality implementation that provides confidence in the foundational Docker infrastructure.
